payload.text: Build a calendar app.

**Defining the Core Objective**

I'm now zeroing in on the project's essence: a React calendar app. TypeScript and Tailwind CSS are the chosen tools. My focus is shaping the app's basic features. Days, events, and leveraging the Gemini API for smart enhancements are key. I see I can probably offer those options!


**Refining Project Scope**

I'm now prioritizing the React calendar's foundational features. A month view, add/edit/delete events, and local storage for event persistence are my focus. I'm building with a lean and beautiful MVP in mind. Then, I will focus on a "Smart Add" button, using the Gemini API. My approach will be a function-calling style, for extracting event details from natural language input. The Gemini model is `gemini-3-flash-preview`.


**Evaluating Tech Stack Choices**

I'm now solidifying the tech choices. React 18+, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS (CDN) are confirmed. I'll use `@google/genai` for the API, `lucide-react` for icons, and lean towards native `Date` objects with a `DateUtils` service to avoid external dependencies. This is to ensure a reliable and zero-dependency solution, unless the prompt assumes a build environment, but most calendar apps use `date-fns`.


**Defining the File Structure**

I'm now laying out the project's file structure, optimizing for clarity and maintainability. I'm focusing on a single-file output, so ESM is my go-to, with native helpers for date management. I'm aiming for clean, reusable components and services. The core files include the entry point, main layout, calendar components, Gemini integration, types, and utility functions, as well as several hooks.


**Planning the Visual Design**

I'm now thinking about the aesthetics. Subtle glassmorphism is appealing, alongside a sticky header and a responsive 7-column grid layout for the calendar. I've broken down the implementation step-by-step: first, metadata, then HTML/index. I'll define `CalendarEvent` types, and build utility functions for dates. Finally, I'll organize services and components, and assemble the app. The Gemini details (API key, `gemini-3-flash-preview`, and schema) are set. I need to get the "blanks" at the start of the month to work, because calendar logic can be a pain!


**Implementing Date Logic**

I'm now focusing on date calculations. I've figured out `firstDayOfMonth` and `daysInMonth`. Events will be filtered using date string comparisons. I'm building a self-contained `utils/date.ts` to replace `date-fns` for portability. Functions will be written for `getDaysInMonth`, `getStartDayOfMonth`, `isSameDay`, and `formatDate`. I want to make the "Smart Add" feature intuitive and handle relative dates correctly, so I'm injecting the current date into the Gemini prompt for resolution. I will set up the files for those components.
